reason: Six of seven criteria PASS — fields added with round-trip, banner string exactly matches, structural integrity holds (UTF-8 OK, py_compile OK, 69420 bytes vs 69597 baseline = within ±2000, all four 'with tab_*:' blocks present, tab_inventory body=19 lines). One criterion UNCERTAIN: criterion 3 requires the banner insertion to be 'purely additive — no existing lines may be deleted or rewritten,' but the builder's own report discloses CRLF→LF line-ending normalization across app_user.py during a Python str-replace rewrite. Content is additive (banner block + helper + FF import only), but every line was technically rewritten by the eol normalization. The previous failure mode (truncation) did NOT recur, so the spirit of the criterion is met; however the letter is borderline. Verdict 'uncertain' rather than 'approve' to flag the rewrite-vs-pure-insertion ambiguity for Neo's review. No revert: practical integrity is intact and verdict is not 'reject'.
